the director has planned scenes inspired by 5 different iconic akshay kumar movies. each scene requires a different number of shooting days, specifically: 4, 6, 8, 10, and 12 days respectively. to optimize the schedule, the director wants to shoot these scenes in such a way that the total number of shooting days is minimized while ensuring that no two consecutive scenes come from movies released in the same decade. represent the decades as sets: {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, and {2010-2019}. given the release years of the movies as 1994, 2004, 2007, 2012, and 2015, devise an optimal shooting schedule that minimizes the total number of shooting days subject to the stated constraint.

the director has a total budget of ₹50 lakhs for the tribute film. the cost per day for shooting each scene is proportional to the complexity of the scene and the iconic status of the akshay kumar movie it represents. let the costs per day for the scenes be ₹1 lakh, ₹1.5 lakhs, ₹2 lakhs, ₹2.5 lakhs, and ₹3 lakhs respectively. formulate a linear programming problem to determine the number of days each scene can be shot to maximize the number of completed scenes without exceeding the budget of ₹50 lakhs. what is the maximum number of scenes that can be completed within the budget?

******
